fields.name: TAC Claim
fields.mappingName: Please select claim type. The certificate must relate to a claim for either a transport accident related injury (TAC claim) or a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim).
fields.alternativeName: Please select claim type. The certificate must relate to a claim for either a transport accident related injury (TAC claim) or a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim).

fields.name: VWA Claim
fields.mappingName: Certificate durations for a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim), unless special reasons apply are up to: -14 days for first certificate (must be issued by a medical practitioner) -28 days for a subsequent certificate.
fields.alternativeName: Certificate durations for a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim), unless special reasons apply are up to: -14 days for first certificate (must be issued by a medical practitioner) -28 days for a subsequent certificate.

fields.name: Confirm Attendance
fields.mappingName: A worker may request an attendance certificate to confirm that they have attended a medical appointment related to their transport accident (TAC) or work-related injury/condition (VWA claim) during work hours. Please complete Sections: 1.Worker details, 2. Diagnosis, Section 5.Treatment details and 6.Certifier declaration
fields.alternativeName: A worker may request an attendance certificate to confirm that they have attended a medical appointment related to their transport accident (TAC) or work-related injury/condition (VWA claim) during work hours. Please complete Sections: 1.Worker details, 2. Diagnosis, Section 5.Treatment details and 6.Certifier declaration

fields.name: Date of Examination Day
fields.mappingName: An examination date must be included. Note: If this certificate refers to a period prior to the date of examination please provide details in additional comments (Section 3. Capacity assessment)
fields.alternativeName: An examination date must be included. Note: If this certificate refers to a period prior to the date of examination please provide details in additional comments (Section 3. Capacity assessment)

fields.name: My Clinical Diagnosis/es
fields.mappingName: A certificate of capacity must specify a clinical diagnosis which identifies the nature of injury or disease and a bodily location. A non-specific diagnosis, such as ‘medical condition’ or symptoms such as sore arm, back pain or stress that do not specify the nature of injury or disease are not acceptable.
fields.alternativeName: A certificate of capacity must specify a clinical diagnosis which identifies the nature of injury or disease and a bodily location. A non-specific diagnosis, such as ‘medical condition’ or symptoms such as sore arm, back pain or stress that do not specify the nature of injury or disease are not acceptable.

fields.name: Check Box1
fields.mappingName: If capacity is affected, further details MUST be provided in this section (consider physical and mental health function). This information will assist in identifying suitable options for your patient to safely stay at work or return to work. If fields are blank this indicates limitations are not applicable. Continue to section 4 if capacity is unaffected.

fields.name: 4A
fields.mappingName: Select at least one certification option and specify the expected duration. Note: Certificate durations for a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim), unless special reasons apply are up to:  -14 days for first certificate (must be issued by a medical practitioner) -28 days for subsequent certificate.
fields.alternativeName: Select at least one certification option and specify the expected duration. Note: Certificate durations for a work-related injury/condition (VWA claim), unless special reasons apply are up to:  -14 days for first certificate (must be issued by a medical practitioner) -28 days for subsequent certificate.

fields.name: Return to work (days)
fields.mappingName: An estimated timeframe will assist with planning for a return to safe work. Research indicates that patients return to work sooner after injury if their healthcare provider advises them of a date to return to work.
fields.alternativeName: An estimated timeframe will assist with planning for a return to safe work. Research indicates that patients return to work sooner after injury if their healthcare provider advises them of a date to return to work.

fields.name: Return to work (weeks)
fields.mappingName: An estimated timeframe will assist with planning for a return to safe work. Research indicates that patients return to work sooner after injury if their healthcare provider advises them of a date to return to work.
fields.alternativeName: An estimated timeframe will assist with planning for a return to safe work. Research indicates that patients return to work sooner after injury if their healthcare provider advises them of a date to return to work.

fields.name: Treatment Plan
fields.mappingName: Outline the treatment plan for your patient including injury management, strategies to increase capacity for work, address return to work barriers and/or prevent recurrence/aggravation of injury. E.g.  Paracetamol x2 4-6 hourly, weekly  physiotherapy (with Joe Smith) for core strengthening, educated patient to remain active and walk 30 minutes per day.
fields.alternativeName: Outline the treatment plan for your patient including injury management, strategies to increase capacity for work, address return to work barriers and/or prevent recurrence/aggravation of injury. E.g.  Paracetamol x2 4-6 hourly, weekly  physiotherapy (with Joe Smith) for core strengthening, educated patient to remain active and walk 30 minutes per day.

fields.name: Provider name address and phone no or practice stamp
fields.mappingName: Work-related injury/condition: The first certificate must be completed by a medical practitioner. Subsequent certificates may be completed by a medical practitioner, physiotherapist, chiropractor or osteopath.                                         Transport accident related injury: The TAC will accept a certificate of capacity from a medical practitioner, dentist, optometrist, physiotherapist, chiropractor, osteopath or podiatrist who is the primary practitioner responsible for treating the injury.

Transport accident related injury: The TAC will accept a certificate of capacity from a medical practitioner, dentist, optometrist, physiotherapist, chiropractor, osteopath or podiatrist who is the primary practitioner responsible for treating the injury.

fields.name: Details of any voluntary work, employment or self-employment you have engaged in
fields.mappingName: Please provide a copy of the completed certificate to your patient and keep one for your records.
fields.alternativeName: Please provide a copy of the completed certificate to your patient and keep one for your records.

fields.name: Mandatory No
fields.mappingName: Please provide a copy of the completed certificate to your patient and keep one for your records. It is your patient’s responsibility to sign the worker declaration (unless it is the first certificate or an attendance certificate) and provide it to their employer (VWA) or claims manager (TAC).
fields.alternativeName: Please provide a copy of the completed certificate to your patient and keep one for your records.
